newsSinceJun6: No ARM-specific fundamental news. The -8.9% June 9 move (on top of ~-13% Friday June 5) is flow-driven: Broadcom's fiscal Q2 revenue miss (June 4) triggered a broad AI-semis multiple compression (SMH -9% Friday), and majority owner SoftBank fell >7% on June 8 in Asia's AI selloff, amplifying ARM selling. An FTC investigation into Arm's licensing practices remains an unresolved overhang but is not new since June 6. ARM's own fundamentals (record FY26 revenue $4.92B, royalty +21%, ~50% hyperscaler CPU compute share) are unchanged; YTD has compressed from ~200%+ to ~175%.

sources.label: CNBC (Jun 4): Broadcom, Micron and ARM sink, leading chip stocks lower
CNBC (Jun 8): Asia tech stocks extend sell-off, SoftBank down over 7%
Quiver Quant: Arm slides as Broadcom-led semiconductor selloff pressures high-multiple AI names

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's down move already priced the post-rerating crowding; today's -8.9% flow-driven washout (no ARM-specific news) removes the impetus to mark it down further and modestly restores asymmetry, but ARM's directness to HBM/packaging/CPO bottlenecks is unchanged, so no upgrade either.
verdicts.bull: A ~22% three-session de-rating with royalty momentum and ~50% hyperscaler CPU share intact is exactly the setup that rebuilds residual alpha in a name that was over-owned a week ago.
verdicts.neutral: Sector multiple compression hit ARM harder than SMH, but its node-level bottleneck exposure remains indirect; the alpha case is better entry, not a better thesis.
verdicts.bear: Broadcom's miss is a genuine read-across question for AI capex breadth, and the FTC licensing probe plus SoftBank-leverage linkage mean further flow-driven downside is possible.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Data-center CPU royalty acceleration (the $2B pipeline converting) re-confirming the Cloud AI ramp despite sector capex doubt.
verdicts.primaryRisk: AI-capex sentiment deteriorates further and SoftBank deleveraging forces continued correlated selling regardless of ARM fundamentals.

verdicts.rankRationale: On a CPO-bottleneck list ARM's exposure is remote and nothing since June 6 changes that; the only update is that the 'very high valuation' risk flag is modestly relieved by a flow-driven -22% three-session drawdown, which doesn't justify a rank move from 92.
verdicts.bull: Valuation risk, the page's stated knock on ARM, is meaningfully less acute after the washout while the custom-silicon ecosystem IP angle is untouched.
verdicts.neutral: CPO cable/optics relevance is still remote; ARM belongs near the bottom of this page whether it trades at 300 or 350.
verdicts.bear: If AI infrastructure spend disappoints, ARM gets hit as a high-multiple proxy without offering any CPO bottleneck scarcity to cushion it.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Evidence that Arm-based custom hyperscaler silicon programs pull through scale-up fabric/CPO attach where ARM IP is in the stack.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued high-multiple AI de-rating; ARM has the beta of the trade with little of this page's specific bottleneck exposure.

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's down was driven explicitly by the 200%+ YTD rerating leaving 'less hidden alpha'; that condition has materially changed — a ~22% flow-driven drawdown (Broadcom read-across plus SoftBank selling, no ARM-specific negative) with record FY26 results intact improves the asymmetry this broad alpha page scores.
verdicts.bull: Sharp sell-off with thesis intact: royalty +21%, data-center royalty doubling, ~50% hyperscaler CPU share, and a $2B server-CPU demand pipeline — the de-crowding is doing the alpha repair for you.
verdicts.neutral: Still a $337B mega-cap up 175% YTD and heavily consensus-owned; the move from 'very crowded' to 'crowded' earns a modest bump, not a re-rank into bottleneck territory.
verdicts.bear: The Broadcom miss may mark the start of an AI-capex digestion phase that compresses ARM's royalty growth assumptions, and the FTC licensing probe could attack the core monetization model.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q1 FY27 print showing data-center royalty momentum surviving the capex scare, plus resolution or de-escalation of the FTC inquiry.
verdicts.primaryRisk: FTC action against the licensing/royalty model, or SoftBank's leveraged AI strategy forcing supply/sentiment overhang on the float.
